source: AGENTS.md section 9 (distributed test constitution), TECHNICAL_STANDARDS.md section 10.2
shard_method_note: No historical per-app duration data exists yet. Split is deterministic by test-collection count (discovery only, zero tests executed, zero DB writes) via django.test.utils.get_runner(settings)().build_suite([label]).countTestCases() per app. Refine to historical-duration once a full-suite run publishes durations, per AGENTS.md section 9 rule 'Split by historical duration when timing data exists.'
test_collection_method: Django DiscoverRunner.build_suite() called per app label, summed; cross-checked against a single build_suite([]) full-repo discovery call, both returned 1429. No test executed, no database created or written to for this count.

secondary_6_core.machine_id_note: No agent has confirmed a 6-core machine this session. Bolt has not reported a logical core count. Ember reported 8 logical cores on machine 'DESKTOP-ODD5QN8' -- the same machine name Bolt reported for his own bootstrap, with no core count given. This is flagged as an open item, see blockers.

linode_1_core.machine_id_note: Confirmed via direct SSH: nproc=1, /proc/cpuinfo processor count=1.
linode_1_core.note: Deliberately the lightest/simplest apps per project convention (feedback_distributed_testing.md), so the shard leaves headroom for the server's live production request handling during the run.
